title: Rapoo Bluetooth 2.4G Wireless Mouse, 2 Bluetooth Channels with 1 USB Receiver Noiseless Mouse, Connect up to 3 Devices, Portable Computer Mice for PC, Tablet, Laptop-Blue
features: 【Easy to Support 3 Device 】Rapoo Bluetooth 3.0, 4.0 Wireless 2.4G technology wireless mouse support 3 devices, two devices by Bluetooth and the third through 2.4GHz USB dongle. Enjoy peace and quiet during your browsing and working experiences. Anti finger-print and sweat proof outer shell, breathable and moisture absorbing, fresh and cool. Suit for Laptop/Desktop/MacBook/NoteBook/ChromeBook.

High precision optical engine, without a mouse pad using the mouse on various surfaces like leather, cloth, paper, desk, wood or resin.

【Convenient & extended battery life】 Rapoo implements low-power technology and a power-saving design with power switches to extend battery life, 9 months battery life under normal conditions. Portable compact size, easy to carry on for office and business trips or travel.

【One year Warranty & 30days Return】 We are the designer and manufacturer for Rapoo mouse and keyboards, we have been engaged to providing the best products to our customers. All the products are hassle-free 30days return and refund or replacement and one year Warranty.

reviews.title: Too cute!
reviews.text: Turned out the same color as I saw on my screen when I ordered it!!! So excited it matched the mouse pad I ordered too! Love it, works perfectly!

reviews.title: Works well, when it connects.  Update:  It loses connection WAY too much
reviews.text:
I like the mouse. Excellent value for money, except for one very annoying flaw. It is constantly disconnecting the bluetooth connection from my new Macbook Pro. It's pretty annoying.

After what feels like only a few minutes of idle time, the mouse bluetoothe disconnects. Then when I move it nothing happens for a couple seconds. The finally, it connects and my laptop tells me the mouse is back, and it works perfectly... until the next time it is idle.

I have use other low cost brands as well as my Apple Magic Mouse (which I don't use as I prefer a track wheel). I have not had any issues with the Bluetooth on those, only this one...

If it were not for the disconnect issue I would rate the mouse a 5 star. Otherwise, I give it a 3. I am still using it for now, but will find a better one...

UPDATE: Okay, I find that I am CONSTANTLY drustrated that this mouse loses connection with my laptop. PITA. Not worth using at any price.

reviews.title: Junk
reviews.text: Even after swapping out the battery, and making sure it’s in pairing mode, it refuses to pair with any device I’ve tried and doesn’t show up in my Bluetooth menu as a visible device. It very briefly showed up on my Macbook as a keyboard, but never as a mouse, and now it won’t even do that. Save your money and get a mouse that will work.

title: Logitech Wireless Mouse M315 - Vivid Violet
description: The feel-good, reliable mouse —that’ll make you forget the touchpad. From surfing the Web to updating your Facebook—you’ll be mousing on more surfaces thanks to Logitech Advanced Optical. No delays, dropouts or hassles. The plug-and-forget nano-receiver keeps you connected with reliable 2.4 GHz wireless. With 12 months of battery life (AA Battery included!), you don’t have to deal with the hassle of frequent battery changes.
features: Logitech Advanced Optical Tracking - Enjoy precise clicking and pointing—on surfaces other optical mice can’t handle—while navigating your favorite websites thanks to Logitech Advanced Optical Tracking.
Logitech Advanced 2.4 GHz wireless connectivity - You get the reliability of a cord with wireless convenience and freedom—fast data transmission and virtually no delays or dropouts.
1-year battery life (AA Battery included!) - You can go for a year without battery hassles. An On/Off switch and smart sleep mode extend battery life, while an indicator light eliminates surprises.
Plug-and-forget nano-receiver - So small and unobtrusive, you can plug it into a USB port and forget about it. Plus, you can say good-bye to worries about broken or lost receivers.
Compact comfort - You’ll enjoy more comfort and ease thanks to this compact mouse’s hand-friendly, contoured design with soft rubber grips and a smooth scroll wheel.

Prepare 2* AAA battery when you first use it(not included);Turn on the switch,use on mouse pad,the mouse and receiver will connect automatically in a few seconds. 2.If not connect,please turn off the mouse switch,pull down the USB receiver,then plug the USB receiver and turn on the mouse switch again, press the 3 buttons(left button,right button,scroll wheel) together at same time for about 3-5 seconds.
Notes:
Cute design with LED headlight,the headlight is formed only because of light transmission,sometimes it hides inside that you cannot see it,this won't affect use. The light is not strong,this could save battery power.

Please use the USB port of your computer to charge it, or use an adapter with an output of no more than 5v to charge it🔋This is a rechargeable mouse. My 10-month-old is a VERY light sleeper! Everything wakes her up, but not these. A major win for me! Highly recommend. 100% I would buy this again! (batteries are not included. reviews.title: Wired + Wireless?  Work for you? Good multimedia controls & phone slot
reviews.text:
Not a fan of wireless accessories (the batteries typically fail without warning, makers want you to be surprised), I made an exception for this pairing model because the keyboard is wired.

PRO
-Ubiquitous AA battery for mouse
-Economical
-Numeric pad
-Multimedia buttons: Volume knob, buttons for mute, pause, previous, next
-A place to put your phone (nice touch!)

CON
-Neither is backlit
-KB not programmable

NEUTRAL
-Non-'clack-y' key design, these are "quiet"
-Some humans will like pink, not widely available, maybe a good gift
-Con (for me) is the wireless mouse

reviews.title: Fun cordless mouse and keyboard
reviews.text: This is a great little retro cordless keyboard and mouse combo. I ordered it because it has just one dongle that both operate from. That gives me more USB ports available for other things. It works great as long as the keyboard and mouse are close to the laptop or the tower. It lags a bit when it’s in a keyboard drawer. I like the compact and portable nature this setup includes!

reviews.title: pink keyboard and wireless mouse
reviews.text: Love the pink color of this retro keyboard and mouse.  Great that the keyboard is spill proof I can have my coffee and not worry about wrecking my keyboard.  Easily plugged right into my all-in-one computer.  Took me a little while to get used to the punk keycaps, and now I love how quiet and comfortable they are to use.  The mouse fits well in my hand, and the wheel rolls back and forth smoothly. Top 5 stars for this one!

title: Logitech M585 Multi-Device Wireless Mouse – Control and Move Text/Images/Files Between 2 Windows and Apple Mac Computers and Laptops with Bluetooth or USB, 2 Year Battery Life, Graphite
description: Talk about a game changer! With Logitech FLOW, M585 Multi-Device has the almost magical capacity to navigate seamlessly on two computers and copy-paste from one to the other – even if you're using a PC and a Mac. It certainly beats sending yourself emails. M585 also comes with a host of other features to improve your productivity, your way: fast and easy scrolling through long web pages; five programmable buttons, including two handy thumb buttons; a 2-year battery life** and a curved, domed shape that supports your palm comfortably during long hours of work or play. Dual connectivity (Bluetooth or 2.4 GHz Logitech Unifying). System Requirements: Required: Bluetooth low energy technologyWindows 10 or later Windows 8Mac OS X 10.10 or lateChrome OSAndroid 5.0 or later|Required: Available USB portWindows 10 or later Windows 8 Windows 7Mac OS X 10.05 or laterChrome OS|Internet connection for software download USB port.
features: CROSS-COMPUTER CONTROL - Game-changing capacity to navigate seamlessly on two computers, and copy-paste text, images, and files from one to the other using Logitech FLOW
DUAL CONNECTIVITY - Use with up to three Windows or Mac computers and laptops via included Unifying receiver or Bluetooth Smart wireless technology
LONG BATTERY LIFE- The Logitech M585 just keeps going, with 2 years of power on a single AA battery
ULTRA-PRECISE SCROLLING- Micro-precision wheel packs more grooves per millimeter to make scrolling through long webpages faster and easier
CURVED DESIGN - Domed shape provides right hand support for your palm comfortably during long hours of work or play
Note : In case of Wireless mouse, the USB receiver will be provided inside or along with the mouse

reviews.title: Excellent travel size and 6 month long battery life!
reviews.text:
Updated 1 year later(May 3, 2020)

Down grading from 5 stars to 3 stars because it turns out there might be a bug in the mouse that once you pair it with 2 different computers over bluetooth, you cannot reset the mouse to use the wireless dongle. There is also no support on the Logitech website - try searching their website for the manual for this mouse, it's absolutely missing. You'll see an FAQ section and some various sections on tech spec, but no setup guide or manual!

*********

If you're tried of replacing batteries, this mouse is for you! I'm impressed by how long a single AA battery lasts in this mouse. I haven't had to replace the battery since getting it 6 months ago even with daily use. The two modes is nice since it lets me connect this mouse to two computers! The bluetooth is strong and connects well to my MacBook Pro, while the included radio frequency receiver is plugged into a iMac and I can switch between computers with a. simple button click.

reviews.title: Great travel mouse!
reviews.text: Love the feel of it. Easy to connect. Small enough to use for travel yet big enough to where it doesn’t hurt to use ergonomically.

reviews.title: WOW! Seamless movement from one computer to the other!
reviews.text:
This device synched to both computers quickly. Download the 'Logi Options' software on both, then enable 'Flow'
Move the mouse off the bottom of one screen & it appears at the top of the other laptop.
Copy a file on computer one, switch to the computer two, right-click->paste. You have copied the file took computer one to computer two.
The mouse itself is legit comfortable, precise asks smooth.
Add 'Flow' and it is also revolutionary.
Highly recommend.

reviews.title: Nice mouse for Mac Pro using Big Sur OS
reviews.text: I was looking for a mouse for my MacPro computer.  It's got 2 buttons on the mouse that I wanted to setup as volume up, and volume down.  But there is no instructions on how to do this, and I can't find any.  I'm using the mouse as a standard mouse with scroll for web pages.  Works perfect for that.

reviews.title: Nice and quiet
reviews.text:
Nice, quiet mouse at a great price. It's on the smaller size, just what I needed for carrying in the computer bag. I have long fingers, but not necessarily large hands, and this fits perfectly for me. The scroll wheel is nice and smooth (and also quiet). You can feel some tactile "knurling" when you scroll, so it's not baby's-butt-smooth, but it's not like scrolling over a circular-saw blade either.

Using the USB dongle, the mouse is very responsive. The Bluetooth connection seemed a little laggy to me. No stars off unless it does the same when I test it on another PC.

I do not intend to install the Logitech Options software so I cannot speak to the usefulness of Flow or reassigning buttons.

Pros
- USB and Bluetooth connectivity
- Can use with up to two devices.
- 7 buttons: L, R, Center, Center-Left, Center-Right, Back, Forward. Eight if you include the PC selector button.
- Very quiet left/right click buttons
- Nearly quiet center click and back/forward buttons
- Uses one AA battery (preferred over non-replaceable battery)
- Battery life advertised at 2 years.
- Has an on/off switch on the bottom

Cons
- Possible laggy response using Bluetooth. I need to test on another PC to be sure.

Other consideration:
- The USB dongle storage is behind the battery door. If you frequently remove the dongle from the PC, this might be a concern for you (it's not a problem for me).

title: Vassink Wireless Mouse Ergonomic Mouse Rechargeable Vertical Optical Mice with USB Receiver, 6 Buttons 2.4GHz Adjustable DPI 800/1200/1600 for PC Computer,Laptop,etc（Black）

★【Efficient and Sensitive-Easy to Use】The ergonomic mouse has 6 convenient buttons design, plug and play; Adjustable 800/1200/1600 DPI mouse movement speed. Optical tracking technology has higher sensitivity than standard optical mouse, usable Smooth and precise tracking on a variety of surfaces.

★【Silent Design-Fashionable Charging】Wireless mouse adopts button-quiet design, low decibel is more suitable for public places such as libraries, dormitories, offices, high-speed rail planes; with built-in lithium battery (500mAh) and USB data cable (150cm) charging device, Make your use more fashionable and convenient

★【Long Life-More Durable】The high-quality optical wireless mouse has been tested for over 5 million mouse clicks. The rechargeable feature makes this wireless mouse more durable.(It is better to use it after receiving the mouse fully charged)

We provide 12 months of worry-free after-sales service. If you encounter any problems with the wireless mouse during use, please feel free to contact us.

reviews.title: Responsiveness is abysmal
reviews.text:
I have a Fellowes mouse pad (vinyl material) and this mouse moves the pointer on it like it is stuck in mud - very slow. Yes, i removed the coated ovals on the bottom. I tried on my desktop protective cover (a softer plastic) and it was even worse. Putting the mouse on paper made it move the mouse pointer faster and smoother but occasionally had hiccups. This is unacceptable for gaming, let alone basic office work. This wasn't even the worse part of this mouse.

The mouse buttons require you to click on the very edge of the button to recognize the click. Because my finger tips reach about 75% of the length I would have to click 3+ times to get Windows 11 to recognize I clicked the button. I made sure to charge the battery to 100% and tried numerous USB ports on my computer and on my docking station to no avail. Would not recommend.

reviews.title: Helped my forearm pain and works as described
reviews.text: Works as described, more natural wrist position and seems to be helping my forearm pain

reviews.title: Very nice mouse!
reviews.text:
I've seen a few of these mice around and this one was inexpensive enough to give the design a try. I normally use only Bluetooth mice because I don't like losing a USB port to a dongle, but made an exception.

I have to say I'm impressed with this device. The performance is rock-solid and it feels great to use. I also like the slightly rubberized finish; feels good in the hand. What I'm doing is alternating between this and a conventional mouse every few days to give my wrist a break. The reduction in wrist discomfort is noticeable.

It's worth losing a USB port to a dongle

reviews.title: Ergonomics
reviews.text: The concept of this mouse is that your hand is in a more natural position when you use it. That part works fine. There is a hesitation that occurs from time to time that will cause you some angst. The mouse button has to wake it from sleep mode, which also takes some getting used to. Overall it is a decent mouse and surprisingly easy to adapt to.

title: Logitech POP Mouse, Wireless Mouse with Customizable Emojis, SilentTouch Technology, Precision/Speed Scroll, Compact Design, Bluetooth, Multi-Device, OS Compatible - Mist
description: Logitech POP Mouse - Silent Wireless Mouse Let personality POP on your deskspace and beyond with POP Mouse, your whisper-quiet, wireless mouse with fresh customization features. Take your self-expression further and download Logitech Software to customize the top button. Give it your own twist by assigning it to send your favorite emoji or another shortcut - such as Snip Screen, Mic Mute or Voice-to-Text. POP Mouse fits comfortably in your hand and slips easily into your pocket, handbag or computer case. SilentTouch technology makes every click whisper-quiet. The portable mouse's SmartWheel stays in sync with you, easily shifting from high-precision to speed scroll mode. Pair POP Mouse with up to 3 different devices at once, and use the Easy-Switch button to toggle between them. Alternatively, activate FLOW and glide between screens as you work, transferring text, files and images with a simple copy and paste. Connect the cordless mouse to your desktop, tablet or phone* using Bluetooth or a Logi Bolt USB receiver which you can purchase separately. With a classic Logitech promise of durability, POP Mouse is your companion for the long haul, with up to 24-month battery life** and auto-sleep power-saving technology that helps it last. Product details: Number of buttons: 4 Top button customizable with Emoji Software Sensor technology: Logitech High Precision Optical Tracking Battery type: 1 x AA battery (included) Battery life: Up to 24 months** Connection type: Bluetooth 5.1 Compatible with Logi Bolt USB receiver Wireless range: 32.81 ft (10 m) Compatible with Windows 10 or later, macOS 10.15 or later, iPadOS, Chrome OS and Android *Windows, macOS, Chrome OS, Android, iPadOS **Battery life may vary based on use and computing conditions.
features: Less is more: Soft grey and natural sand colors kiss on an elegant, minimalist palette, allowing you to make an understated impact with POP Silent Mouse in Mist
Bold, comfy, quiet: A compact and comfy wireless mouse with up to 24-month battery life that fits snugly in your palm, handbag and computer case, with SilentTouch Technology for whisper-quiet working
The emoji button: Press the colorful mouse's magic middle button to open a menu of emojis to use in your chats, or give it your own shortcut using Logitech Software (Windows and macOS only)
Shift gears on the fly: As if reading your mind, POP Mouse’s SmartWheel shifts from high-precision tracking to speed scroll mode, helping you whizz through long documents with one confident flick
FLOW between worlds: Connect to up to 3 devices on Windows, Mac, Chrome OS or Android using Bluetooth and glide between screens using the Easy-Switch button or FLOW
Aesthetic pair: Match your POP Mouse with the fun and bouncy typing experience of POP Keys wireless mechanical keyboard, with customizable emoji keycaps and cutting-edge media keys

Plastic parts in POP Mouse include certified post consumer recycled plastic (Blast: 46%; Daydream, Heartbreaker and Mist: 29%; Cosmos: 27%*). *Excludes printed wiring assembly (PWA) plastics, and packaging.
Certified carbon neutral: the carbon impact of POP Mouse has been reduced to zero.

reviews.title: Horrible Connection
reviews.text: I’ve tried this mouse with multiple devices now and it disconnects and reconnects every five seconds. It’s basically impossible to use.

reviews.title: Quiet and super fast Bluetooth setup - no Emoji function with iPadOS
reviews.text: Within a minute of taking this out of the box, I had it paired with my iPad. It was seriously so easy to just pair and go, and I love how it can be paired with three different things (just push a little button on the bottom of the mouse to switch between computers/iPads). It feels okay in my hand - not the highest quality I’ve seen (plastic is very basic and it is pretty lightweight as well), but it doesn’t feel cheap. The color is so fun, which is the main reason I got it. Also it is very quiet and doesn’t have any loud clicking. The one thing to mention is that the circle button under the scrolling wheel doesn’t have any functionality with an iPad. Nothing was happening when I kept clicking it to try and use the emoji shortcut, so after heading over to Logitech’s website i saw “*Emojis and software are currently supported on Windows® and macOS only.” So maybe iPadOS will eventually support this capability, but as of now it is useless. Overall I still like the mouse a lot for it’s ease of use, quietness, and color.

reviews.title: Beautiful little mouse with a pink color that truly POPS!
reviews.text:
THE GOOD:
---------------
* The pink color of this mouse really POPS!
* Operation is smooth, just like any mouse.
* Multi device with a click of button underneath the mouse. You can save up to 3 devices.
* Battery is included and is rated for 2 yrs. Wow, we'll see.
* Emoji dedicated center button for chats.
* Weighs 2.9 ounces.

THE BAD:
------------
* No information in box whatsoever about the emoji software. You'll need to go to Logitech's website and do some digging. I didn't bother since my little girl won't be chatting.
* Bluetooth only. No mentioning in the manual whether or not it works with their unifying receiver.

I got this mouse because my little girl loves pink, and the red coral color here truly pops! She does basic homework stuff on the pc. She's not at that chatting age yet, so I don't really care or tested the emoji feature. Logitech didn't make it easy for you to test it out anyway, so I didn't bother. I'll go 4-Stars overall.

reviews.title: great trustworthy brand, ergonomically comfortable with hours of use.
reviews.text: we have used logitech for so many of our technology needs over the decades and we love the new wireless mouse, which was super easy to connect.  the mouse can connect up to 3 appliances including tablets.  works for both android and apple.  very effortless to use and silent. the emoji button is not what we were looking for, but a plus.  Not being a Y gen - lol, it took me a while to figure how to work the emoji button.  again, not a key aspect for us.  Curious to test that the battery will last for 1 year as claimed, but easy to take off the 'color' plate and replace the AA battery.  very happy with the color.  fun and ergonomically comfortable.

reviews.title: I like the mouse
reviews.text: It is a piece of cotton candy fluff in my hands. I can move quickly over my laptop screen and edit easily. It paired immediately too. For those who like social media, you get an emoji button too. You just push on the wheel and hit the right part of the mouse top. I do not like social media anymore. But others do all the time.  They will love the ease of this mouse.

reviews.title: The mouse and keyboard work on the same USB plug.

reviews.title: Usable keyboard. Mouse is a little problematic.
reviews.text: The keyboard is good and more ergonomic than others but lack of function keys makes less than desirable. The mouse just kept, intermittently disconnecting so was unusable.

reviews.title: Comfortable to use
reviews.text: I didn't have any expectations for this wireless keyboard and mouse set. I just wanted it to sync up easily to the PC and it did. More than that, it is a surprisingly comfortable keyboard to work with. The keys are responsive and accurate. I can put it at an angle that I like. It is easy to store away. I just really like it more than I had expected. I may not have had particular expectations. I just wanted to try out a keyboard/mouse set on a PC that wasn't getting much use, but I may end up putting this set with my main PC set up. I like it that much.

reviews.title: Plug n Play
reviews.text: This is a nice keyboard mouse combo. Set up was simple. There is one dongle for both the keyboard and the mouse. The keyboard is quiet, comfortable and a good size. The mouse is responsive. Time will tell how long it lasts but for now I am pleased with its performance.

reviews.title: A very nice mouse.
reviews.text:
I tried to use both of the most recent Apple mouse products with my Macbook Pro and never could get the hang of either one of them. I tried configuring them in the applications folder but never could get it right plus neither one of them was comfortable. One had a ball and the other newer one did not. Usually I really like apple products but not this time. I am happy to report this Logitech worked right out of the box after I plugged in the USB thingy. i didn't even have to set it up. I do like the roller ball. I think this mouse can do more than I know about, but am just happy it isn't changing the page I am on constantly. I got the one that was painted blue and green because it was about 6.00 cheaper than the solid colored ones. I like the design now that I have it. Since my laptop only has two USB plug ins, I bought the Amazon USB hub with three additional spots. Am happy with that too.
UPDATE 11-08-14
All of a sudden I am getting these messages saying the battery is critically low. To start with I was changing the batteries every time but it was happening way too fast so I bought a battery checker. Turns out my batteries are fine. Testing 100 %. the mouse continue to operate even though I continue receiving low battery warnings. Don't know how this is going to play out but it is the first sign of trouble. Now I just take battery out. Test it and when it continues to say 100 % I put it back in and the message goes away for a couple of days. Aggravating. I am amending star count down to 4 stars. Can't change stars.

reviews.title: Works great - great little wireless mouse.
reviews.text: I got this for my daughters birthday - she loved the design.  It synced right up and worked immediately.  It worked great with her computer - she really liked he feel of the mouse.  It is easy to pack away and travel with as well.

reviews.title: The middle click and scroll wheel work great! Microsoft 4000 is smoother and more comfortable though.
reviews.text:
I own this mouse as well as the Microsoft Wireless Mobile Mouse 4000 now. I find them to be head to head competitors, so here's a quick comparison.

Logitech:
-Somewhat of a loud click, doesn't bother me personally, but it is obviously louder
-The harder you flick the scroll wheel the more it'll spin. There is resistance though and I feel like it's easier to use than the Microsoft mouse.
-It looks cool and functionality is fine. The middle click is easy to use and the left/right of the wheel to move back or forward in a web browser works and isn't annoyingly used by accident.

Microsoft:
-Quiet click and smoother sliding on the desk
-The wheel stops once your finger leaves it. You can't just flick and go. Scrolling seems more precise with this one, but I actually prefer the Logitech as I feel like I have more functional control with it.
-There is a side button that moves you backward in a web browser, the wheel left and right tilting doesn't seem to do anything.
-More comfortable in my hand.

Bottom line: Microsoft Wireless Mobile Mouse 4000 is smoother and more comfortable, but Logitech is more functional, especially for the middle click and scroll wheel. I was very skeptical about the benefits of this "free-wheel" but I love it now. If you're not a big scroll wheel and middle click enthusiast go with the Microsoft, otherwise the Logitech is for you.

reviews.title: Five Stars
reviews.text: Great.

Comfort for Either Hand
Work anywhere with the world's most advanced tracking.
Comfort and durability
Modern design and premium finish provides comfort for either hand. Symmetrical shape and unique rubber grips give you control and cushioning.
Snap in and Go
Simply snap in the transceiver for convenient on-the-go storage.
Reliable Wireless
Microsoft 2.4 ghz wireless delivers a reliable connection with up to a 30-foot range.
8-Month Battery Life
Use your mouse without power interruption for up to 8 months. The snap-in transceiver automatically turns the mouse on and off, and the led battery status indicator lets you know when replacement is needed.
What is BlueTrack?
BlueTrack Technology works on more surfaces than both optical and laser mice. It uses high-angle imaging optics to generate an accurate picture of the surface.
System Requirements
Windows 7, Windows Vista, Windows XP (excluding Windows XP 64-bit)
Windows 7, Windows Vista, Windows XP (excluding Windows XP 64-bit)

reviews.title: Good but wanted more features
reviews.text:
I bought this in a time crunch. My wired mouse was getting hard to use because the scroll wheel had shifted out of place. I had several wireless mice around, but none of them still had their wireless receiver. And they were before the days of Bluetooth. So, I had was in the market for a new one.

This one is nice. It fits the hand well and all. But, I realized that it did not have the features that I was used to. First, it is not a quiet mouse. I did not notice at first, but when it penetrated my fog of concentration, I could not unhear it. Click. Click. Click! And, this was not great for conference calls either.

The other issue was that it tracked slower than I would like. I was still using my wired mouse at this point, so I did not want to change mouse settings via software. I really missed having a dpi button on my mouse. This is important to me as I use a laptop on an extended display to a 2nd monitor. So, I need to be able to zip from one end of the screen the other - quick enough to unmute and answer a question during a conference call.

Have to click to wake up the mouse kept catching my attention - which means it was on it's way to annoying me... I have worked with computers for so long, it's like an extension of me. It needs to be intuitive to use and not interrupt my flow.

And, at this point, I figured out that I would need to use two different computers at once. And, while I could pair this wireless mouse with a wired one, the USB ports required were a challenge. My newer computer has no USB C ports at all. So, this limited my set-up options. Secondly, I now had 2 mice crowding limited desk space.

So, I learned that there was such a thing as multi-device mice. And, I found one that can switch between two computers and a iPad - with a simple LED indicator so that you know which device you are controlling. I bought the also designed to be silent and to switch between different dpi. All these features and it was only $3 more.

This is what I bought to replace this one - Arteck Multi-Device Wireless Bluetooth Mouse with Nano USB Receiver Ergonomic Right Hand Silent Clicking for Computer Desktop PC Laptop Mac iPad and Windows 10/8 iPad OS Build in Rechargeable Battery

So, to sum it up, I would say that I need the speed and responsiveness of a gamer's mouse with the silent clicks and 3 device connects like an office worker. So, this mouse is ok. But, if you are an advance or intense user, you may find that it does meet your needs.
